_comment: WO-127 ship spec (charter 40 sec. 4): the logic-analyzer jig ships the SAME families a fleet target ships -- the elec bring-up BOM, the real BoardOutline (80x50) through the deterministic board leg, the firmware product, and (under --emit-profile debug) its own harness family. The jig is a debuggable design like any other; the recursion stops here (no jig-for-the-jig).

assembly.description: Tap header, 2x8 2.54mm shrouded box header -- MATES the target board's debug-profile header (std.elec tap_header_2x08_254)
USB Type-C 2.0 receptacle, 16-pin -- bus power in, capture stream out
Capture MCU: PIO parallel capture + native USB device
SWD debug header (the jig is itself debuggable)
Local HF bypass, one per RP2040 power pin (iovdd/dvdd/usb_vdd/adc_avdd/qspi_vdd/vreg_vin)

LaJigCapture._comment: WO-127 deliverable 2. AD-22: the tree is SOURCE (application + PIO program + contract header). Nothing here invokes a compiler and no ELF is pinned, so the package ships an honest elf: null with the reason below -- never a fabricated image. NOTE (F-WO127-6): the firmware family consumes a content-addressed {filename: content} tree with no path-reference form, so the application source's single home is THIS tree; keeping a second copy under firmware/app/ would be exactly the desync trap the repo's no-duplication rule forbids.

files.app/la_jig8_capture.pio:
; la_jig8 PIO capture program (WO-127 deliverable 2).
;
; One instruction per sample: shift all 8 channels in parallel off the
; input pin group and autopush to the DMA FIFO. The sample interval is
; therefore exactly the PIO clock divider -- no CPU, no interrupt
; jitter, no drift. This is the whole reason the RP2040 is the right
; part for this board.

.program la_jig8_capture
.wrap_target
    in pins, 8      ; 8 channels -> ISR; autopush at 8 bits fires the DMA
.wrap

LaJigCapture.elf_absent_reason: no application image is pinned for this design: the WO-37 realizer generates the BSP/contract layer only and AD-22 forbids invoking a compiler at realize time. The application source ships in this tree (app/main.c, app/la_jig8.h, app/la_jig8_capture.pio); link it against the shipped BSP with the RP2040 SDK and pin the resulting bytes via FirmwareArtifact.elf_content_hash to ship an image.

debug._comment: WO-125 (D237.2): consumed ONLY by `ship --emit-profile debug`. The jig is a debuggable design like any other fleet member -- when IT misbehaves you probe it the same way it lets you probe others. The explicit tap wins channel 0; the rest fill from claim-named nets ranked rails < clocks < buses < rest.

taps.why: bring-up: 'is the USB pair even toggling?' is the first question when the jig will not enumerate (claim usb_diff_z0 names this net)
